sections.paragraph:
(kindred to the verb מָלַט, which see) pr. to smoothen; hence-

(1) to polish, to sharpen a sword, Eze 21:14, 33 Eze 21:33.

(2) to make any one’s head smooth, i.e. to make bald, to tear out, to pluck the hair, in contending, chastening, Neh 13:25 in scorn, Isa 50:6 (where מֹרְטִים are those who pluck the beard); in mourning, Ezr 9:3.- Eze 29:18, כָּל־כָּתֵף מְרוּטָה “every shoulder was peeled,” i.e. with carrying burdens.

Niphal, to become bald, Lev 13:40, 41 Lev 13:41.

Pual-

(1) to be polished (used of metal), 1Ki 7:45.

(2) to be sharp, as a sword. Part. מֹורָֽמָּה for מְמֹרָטָה (with Dag. f. euphon.), Ezek: 21:15 , 16 Eze 21:16. Hither many refer עַם מֹורָט Isa 18:2, Isa 18:7, for מְמֹרָט a sharp people, i.e. fierce, vehement (compare חָדַד No. 2). But see above under the word מֹורָט .
